title: Pungent Light Indian Pork Back Ribs Curry
description: A flavorful and aromatic Indian-inspired curry dish featuring pork back ribs, perfect for a light brunch, served in a stone bowl. This recipe is shojin-friendly and features a blend of spices and herbs.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together curry powder, garam masala, turmeric, and cayenne pepper.
Add the pork back ribs to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and sear the pork back ribs until browned on all sides.
Transfer the pork back ribs to a stone bowl and add the coconut milk and yogurt.
Cover the stone bowl with a lid and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve hot.
Serve the curry in the stone bowl, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
